history.titleZh: GeForce NOW在游戏开发者大会展现新貌
history.descriptionZh:
英伟达GeForce NOW云游戏服务本周登陆旧金山游戏开发者大会（GDC），在开发者构建游戏未来的同时，GeForce NOW正将这些体验交付给全球玩家。本次更新带来了更流畅的性能、更便捷的游戏发现方式以及一系列新的云游戏大作。

**核心升级与技术创新**

此次更新的核心在于提升用户体验的便捷性与流畅度。在**游戏发现**方面，服务引入了全新的应用内标签功能，专门用于标识已关联的Xbox Game Pass和Ubisoft+订阅账户中的游戏。此举旨在解决玩家在多平台订阅中难以追踪可用内容的痛点。关联账户后，GeForce NOW应用内的游戏图标上将直接显示清晰标签，让会员一目了然地看到可通过现有订阅游玩哪些游戏，实现无缝跳转。

**虚拟现实（VR）体验获得显著增强**。自3月19日起，GeForce NOW对包括Apple Vision Pro、Meta Quest和Pico在内的支持设备，将其VR流媒体帧率从原有的60帧每秒（fps）提升至90 fps，此升级面向Ultimate会员。更高的帧率意味着更流畅、响应更迅速且更逼真的VR体验，无论是穿梭于霓虹灯闪烁的街道追击敌人，还是探索遥远的外星世界，临场感都将大幅提升。

**账户联动生态持续扩展**。继今年1月国际消费电子展（CES）上宣布支持Gaijin账户单点登录后，GeForce NOW计划在未来几个月内增加对GOG平台的账户链接及游戏库同步支持。这将进一步简化玩家管理多个游戏平台库的流程。

**游戏阵容扩充与发布同步**

GeForce NOW的游戏库持续快速增长，并致力于实现新游戏的“首发即云化”。已确认将在发布时即加入云游戏阵容的包括：
*   **《CONTROL Resonant》**：Remedy即将推出的动作冒险RPG，融合超自然能力与面临现实扭曲宇宙威胁的曼哈顿。
*   **《Samson: A Tyndalston Story》**：由Liquid Swords开发的硬派动作清版游戏，设定于Tyndalston市。

此外，部分Xbox游戏将加入“安装即玩”库，例如Double Fine Productions的《Brutal Legend》和Compulsion Games的《Contrast》，为会员在流媒体游戏之外，提供下载安装自有游戏的更多灵活性。

**本周新增游戏**阵容包括卡普空的《怪物猎人 Stories 3：扭曲映照》等五款作品。同时，多款新游戏标注为“GeForce RTX 5080就绪”，包括《约翰·卡朋特的毒毒突击队》、《1348 Ex Voto》以及《贪婪之秋：垂死世界 1.0》等，展现了其对最新硬件的优化支持。

**重点内容与专属奖励**

*   **《堡垒之夜》“拯救世界”模式免费化**：该模式将于4月16日正式转为免费游玩，预注册于3月12日开启。在GeForce NOW上，玩家可直接流畅云端体验，无需等待更新或补丁下载，低延迟流媒体确保建造、射击和陷阱布置等操作在所有受支持设备上都灵敏快捷。
*   **Ultimate会员专属奖励**：本周，Ultimate会员可领取EA《战地6》的专属“进阶幽暗士兵皮肤”，为角色增添前线战士风范。该奖励通过GeForce NOW账户门户领取，在EA官网兑换，有效期至4月12日或赠完即止。

**总结**

通过此次GDC期间的系列公告，GeForce NOW强化了其作为高端云游戏平台的价值主张：**简化游戏获取与管理、提升核心技术性能（特别是VR）、扩大首发游戏阵容、并通过专属奖励增强会员价值。** 从便捷的订阅游戏标识到90 fps的VR流媒体，再到与PC同步发售的3A大作，GeForce NOW正持续推动云游戏体验向更无缝、更高质量的方向发展。

history.title: Acer Swift 16 AI Review: A Premium contender with a curious twist
history.description: Acer’s Swift 16 AI blends a premium design, an OLED screen, and the latest Intel silicon, though its massive haptic touchpad isn’t the selling point it’s made out to be.
history.content: Acer’s Swift 16 AI (starting at $1,599, $1,899 as tested) arrives with the air of a flagship laptop: a premium metal chassis, Intel’s new “Panther Lake” platform, and what Acer calls the world’s largest haptic touchpad. It also includes an essential at this price point: a stunning OLED display. On paper, it has all the makings of a top ultrabook, but the real question is whether it stands apart from the heavy hitters in this segment, particularly the 15-inch Apple MacBook Air.Design of the Acer Swift 16 AIFirst off, note that the preproduction model Acer sent for review has a lighter color scheme than production models, which will be a darker gray and feature gold highlights on the lid, with the Acer logo on the left. It offers a modern vibe either way, with thin display bezels and a sleek aluminum chassis that looks and feels premium.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The sleek chassis suffers from some lateral flex – picking up this laptop by a corner wouldn’t be a good idea – but the lid is impressively rigid. Acer says the laptop does pass MIL-STD-810 testing for temperatures, humidity, vibration, and moderate drops, suggesting it’s more durable than it seems. The lid folds open 180 degrees, a feature I always appreciate, and it can also be opened one-handed.At 13.99 x 9.66 x 0.59 inches, the Swift’s footprint is similar to the Dell 16 Premium (14.1 x 9.4 x 0.75 inches) but is larger than the 15.3-inch Apple MacBook Air (13.4 x 9.35 x 0.45 inches). Acer kept the weight down to 3.42 pounds, undercutting the 4.65-pound Dell and being marginally heavier than the 3.3-pound MacBook.Port selection includes two Thunderbolt 4 (USB-C), USB-A (5 Gbps), and HDMI 2.1 on the left and another USB-A (10 Gbps), a 3.5 mm audio jack, and a microSD card reader on the right. It’s a solid variety; having two USB-A ports is a luxury on a premium laptop, which often only sport USB-C (the MacBook Air and Dell’s XPS line come to mind). For wireless, it supports the latest Wi-Fi 7 and Bluetooth 5.4 standards from a Killer BE1775s card.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)SpecificationsSwipe to scroll horizontallyCPUIntel Core Ultra X7 358HGraphicsIntel Arc B390Memory32GB LPDDR5X-7467Storage1TB SSD (WD PC SN5000S)Display16-inch, OLED, 16:10, 2880 x 1800, 120 Hz, touchNetworkingKiller Wi-Fi 7 BE1775s, Bluetooth 5.4Ports2x Thunderbolt 4, USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, USB-A 3.2 Gen 2, HDMI 2.1, 3.5 mm audio jack, microSD card readerCamera1080p IRBattery70 WhPower Adapter100 W (Type-C)Operating SystemWindows 11 HomeDimensions (WxDxH)13.99 x 9.66 x 0.59 inchesWeight3.42 poundsPrice (as configured)$1,899.99Today's best Acer Swift 16 AI (Intel) deals $889.99View $899.99ViewLow Stock $1,395ViewWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricesProductivity Performance on the Acer Swift 16 AIOur review sample features a Core Ultra X7 358H CPU, 32GB of RAM, and a 1TB SSD. The “Panther Lake” CPU features 16 cores (four Performance, eight Efficient, and four Low-Power Efficient) and Arc B390 integrated graphics, the latter being a key selling point for the X7/X9 chips over the standard H-class, which rely on Intel Graphics.The laptops we’re using for comparison are listed below. This mix represents mid-to-upper-end productivity laptops with strong CPUs. The Zenbook’s Core Ultra X9 is similar to the X7 in our Acer, but it’s clocked slightly higher.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsSwipe to scroll horizontallyApple MacBook Air (M4, 2025)Apple M4 (10-core)Asus Zenbook Duo (2026)Intel Core Ultra X9 388HDell 16 PremiumIntel Core Ultra 7 255HImage 1 of 4(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)In the Geekbench CPU test, the Acer posted middling scores, delivering 2,789 points in the single-core portion, to practically tie the Dell but trailing the Zenbook (3,031) and particularly the MacBook (3,780). Its 15,926-point multi-core showing slotted towards the bottom of the group, surprisingly ahead of the MacBook (14,924) but trailing the Zenbook and Dell by a sizable margin.In our 25GB file transfer test, Acer rebounded with a leading score of 1,745 MBps, outdoing the Dell (1,531 MBps) and leaving the Apple and last-place Asus well behind.Finally, the Acer demonstrated middling performance in our 4K-to-1080p Handbrake transcoding test, completing the render in 4 minutes and 25 seconds. The Zenbook was within seconds (4:22) while the Dell proved the quickest (3:50) and the Macbook was the slowest (4:52).We stress test ultrabooks by running Cinebench 2024 ten times sequentially to simulate extended CPU load. The Swift 16 started with 948 points, dipped to 762 points on the second run, but then stabilized around the 900-point mark for the remaining runs, suggesting no obvious performance throttling once the fans spin up.Display on the Acer Swift 16 AIOLED screens rarely fail to impress and this one certainly doesn’t, showing off the rich colors and inky contrast the technology is renowned for. The glossy surface is more reflective than I’d hoped, but the 120 Hz refresh rate makes for smooth scrolling. The refresh rate automatically switches to 60 Hz on battery to conserve power. Note that while the surface supports touch, it does not support the included stylus, which only works on the touchpad.Watching Silo on Apple TV is a treat on this Acer. While the show’s overall drab tones rarely gave the OLED display a chance to show off its color, it produced immaculate detail in the shadowy and dimly-lit scenes that dominate the show, adding extra depth and an immersion factor to scenes that can’t be experienced without a top-tier display. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Acer’s screen showed remarkable color coverage, with full coverage of DCI-P3, next to the Zenbook’s 87.2% and the MacBook’s 83.5%. It also showed good if not exceptional brightness for an OLED panel, averaging 364 nits, nearly tying the Dell’s 367 nits but trailing the Zenbook’s 476 nits.Keyboard and Touchpad on the Acer Swift 16 AIThis Acer’s full-size keyboard is a joy to type on, with soft but precise feedback and ample up-and-down travel. Bold white backlighting is easily visible in all lighting conditions. The number pad is small, and the arrow keys aren’t all the same size, but the rest of the layout poses no surprises. I nearly reached my top typing speed in MonkeyType, about 114 words per minute with perfect accuracy, on the first try. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Acer calls this the world’s largest haptic touchpad, measuring 6.9 by 4.3 inches. To put that in perspective, it’s only marginally smaller than an Apple iPad mini. For daily use, I found the size unnecessary as my fingers favored the center of the pad, rarely venturing towards the edges except when I used multi-finger sweeping gestures, which felt downright luxurious because of all the space. The haptic feedback accurately simulates a physical click mechanism and can be adjusted for intensity in the Windows Touchpad settings app.The real reason this touchpad is so large is because it supports MPP styli, and Acer includes one in the box. The idea is this could be used as a drawing pad. The touchpad has the same 16:10 aspect ratio as the screen to make this as intuitive as possible.In practice, I didn’t find the stylus useful. It’s designed well enough – it’s about the size of a real pen, supports tilt, features two buttons, and is powered by a AAAA battery – but writing and doodling on the pad is awkward. Palm rejection didn’t always register, so I ended up not wanting to rest my palm on the surface while writing, which felt unnatural. I had a hard time writing legibly or getting the brush strokes I wanted in Photoshop either way. Overall, the experience felt imprecise and limiting. It could be handy for signing the occasional digital document, but artists would be much better off with a USB drawing pad or simply buying a tablet or 2-in-1 convertible laptop to begin with. Think of thwe stylus as more of a bonus than a feature you’d depend on for real work.Audio on the Acer Swift 16 AIAcer puts on a solid audio performance from the dual downward-firing speakers under the palm rest. Listening to the acoustic “Too Late for Love” by Deep Chills, I heard clear instrument separation and noticeable bass in drum hits. The vocals sounded a bit strained at upper volume levels, but the DTS: X Ultra app equalizers mitigated that most of the time. The equalizers are essential for bass; the sound is nearly flat with them disabled. The music preset sounded best to my ears, with the Movie preset a bit too distant. I tried making a custom preset, but the Music preset always seemed to be what I went back to – it’s balanced just right.The sound also did Silo justice, with detailed vocals and convincing virtual surround-sound effects. The volume seems to top out around 75 to 80%, though. My ears couldn’t tell whether the sound actually got louder beyond that.Upgradeability of the Acer Swift 16 AIEight Torx screws secure the Swift 16 AI’s bottom cover. Once removed, the only place to find purchase is along the display hinge, where I was able to wedge a plastic trim tool and pop the retainer clips. This took a lot more effort than I expected. I then worked up each edge until all the clips released, taking my time as to not break the clips. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Serviceable components are limited to the M.2 SSD and the battery, with all other components soldered or integrated. Having so little upgradeability isn’t surprising in this class. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Battery Life on the Acer Swift 16 AIOur battery test consists of web browsing, running OpenGL tests, and streaming videos with the screen at 150 nits while connected to Wi-Fi. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)With 12 hours and 12 minutes of runtime, the Acer disappointed, only outlasting the power-hungry Dell (7:15). It fell over two hours short of the dual-screen Asus and three hours shy of the MacBook. While the Acer’s time is hardly unusable, the bar has been raised beyond the time it showed here.Heat on the Acer Swift 16 AIThe Swift 16 AI’s cooling system uses two fans that exhausts towards the display hinge. These operated quietly for daily tasks. While audible under load, the noise level is unobtrusive and shouldn’t disturb others.During our Cinebench stress test, we measured peak surface temperatures of 92 degrees F between the keyboard’s G and H keys, 78 degrees on the touchpad, and 95 degrees on the bottom towards the hinge. Meanwhile, the Core Ultra X7 358H CPU ran at an average of 80 degrees C. Overall, this is more than acceptable thermal performance.Webcam on the Acer Swift 16 AIAcer’s 1080p webcam delivers an average picture. The 1080p resolution looks sharper than the 720p fare that was common years ago, but still showed soft details. As an example, I know I have some soft lines in my face and they weren’t visible. (Maybe I should be relieved.) Colors and clarity suffer in lower light, but you’ll still be recognizable. The cam offers an IR sensor for facial recognition and a sliding privacy shutter.Software and Warranty on the Acer Swift 16 AIAcer Sense is the most important app, launchable with the shortcut key along the top row. The app has diagnostics, software updates, an optimized battery charge mode, a blue light filter, and adaptive brightness settings. You can also set the performance profile – silent, normal (the default), or performance.Further personalization settings can be found in the Acer User Sensing app, with a proximity sensor enabling auto-lock when stepping away or wake on approach, andoptional screen dimming when you look away, a break reminder, and a screen distance reminder.Acer preloads a fair number of trial apps, like DropBox, which I uninstalled immediately. There shouldn’t be any bloatware on a premium laptop.Acer covers the Swift 16 AI with a standard one-year warranty.Acer Swift 16 AI ConfigurationsWe reviewed the Swift 16 AI in configuration SF16-71T-75XY, which will be available online in the Acer store in mid-March 2026 for $1,899.99. It includes a Core Ultra X7 358H processor, Intel Arc B390 integrated graphics, 32GB of RAM, and a 1TB SSD. It also has the same 2880 x 1800 OLED touch screen that is standard across all models.Acer will also offer models priced on either side of our unit, both through Best Buy. A $1,599 model SF16-71T-70PN has the same specifications as our review model except half the RAM (16GB), while a $1,999 model SF16-71T-97GV bumps the CPU to a Core Ultra X9 388H.All models include a carry sleeve, which wasn’t included with our review sample, and a stylus for the touchpad.Bottom LineAcer’s Swift 16 AI is an appealing premium ultrabook for buyers willing to spend beyond the basics. Its metal chassis, vivid OLED display, excellent keyboard, and surprisingly capable speakers all contribute to a high-end vibe, and Acer’s practical port selection means most users won’t need adapters.While the battery life lands in the “good, not great” category – a not unexpected result given the OLED display – it falls short of class leaders. Performance is good, with a meaningful generation-on-generation improvement from the “Panther Lake” chip, and Acer’s thermal management is well-designed.The “world’s largest” haptic touchpad is the Swift 16 AI’s most unusual flourish, though in practice the sheer size is more of a curiosity than a must-have. The included stylus works, but the overall experience was imprecise and awkward for me, and hard to treat as a headline feature. It’s best considered a bonus than a reason to buy.So, is the Swift 16 AI worth the asking price? For Windows users who want a sleek, premium 16-inch travel companion, it certainly can be. Its only major misstep is the oversized touchpad and stylus pairing, but that doesn’t seem to carry a significant price premium. Either way, for similar money, Apple’s 15-inch MacBook Air remains our top large-screen ultrabook pick for those who can live in macOS. And while we only had the M4 model to compare the Acer to during this review, the new M5 Air is likely to be even more impressiuve when it comes to performance and longevity. But for die-hard Windows users, Acer’s Swift 16 AI is a polished, capable machine that should leave most buyers satisfied.
history.author: Charles Jefferies
history.titleZh: 宏碁Swift 16 AI评测：高端竞争者，特色引人瞩目
history.descriptionZh:
宏碁全新推出的Swift 16 AI笔记本电脑（起售价1599美元，测试机型售价1899美元）以旗舰姿态登场：采用优质金属机身、英特尔新一代“Panther Lake”平台，并配备了宏碁宣称的全球最大尺寸触感触控板。在这个价位段，一块出色的OLED显示屏也必不可少。从纸面参数看，它具备了顶级超极本的所有要素，但真正的问题在于，它能否在与同级别强劲对手的竞争中脱颖而出，特别是15英寸的苹果MacBook Air。

**宏碁Swift 16 AI的设计**
首先需要注意的是，宏碁送测的工程样机颜色比量产机型更浅，量产版将为深灰色，顶盖带有金色点缀，左侧印有宏碁Logo。无论哪种配色，它都提供了现代感，拥有窄边框显示屏和外观质感俱佳的流线型铝制机身。

纤薄的机身存在一些横向弯曲——不建议握住一角拿起这台笔记本——但顶盖的刚性令人印象深刻。宏碁表示该笔记本通过了MIL-STD-810标准中关于温度、湿度、振动和适度跌落测试，表明其耐用性比看上去更好。屏幕可开合至180度，这是一个备受好评的设计，并且支持单手开合。

Swift 16 AI的尺寸为13.99 x 9.66 x 0.59英寸，与戴尔16 Premium（14.1 x 9.4 x 0.75英寸）相近，但比15.3英寸的苹果MacBook Air（13.4 x 9.35 x 0.45英寸）更大。宏碁将其重量控制在3.42磅，轻于4.65磅的戴尔机型，比3.3磅的MacBook略重。

接口方面，左侧配备了两个雷电4（USB-C）、一个USB-A（5 Gbps）和一个HDMI 2.1；右侧则提供了另一个USB-A（10 Gbps）、一个3.5毫米音频插孔和一个microSD读卡器。这是一个相当全面的组合；在高端笔记本上配备两个USB-A接口堪称奢侈，这类产品通常只提供USB-C接口（例如MacBook Air和戴尔XPS系列）。无线方面，它通过Killer BE1775s网卡支持最新的Wi-Fi 7和蓝牙5.4标准。

**规格配置**
*   **处理器**：英特尔酷睿Ultra X7 358H
*   **显卡**：英特尔锐炫 B390（集成）
*   **内存**：32GB LPDDR5X-7467
*   **存储**：1TB SSD（WD PC SN5000S）
*   **显示屏**：16英寸OLED，16:10比例，2880 x 1800分辨率，120 Hz刷新率，触控
*   **网络**：Killer Wi-Fi 7 BE1775s，蓝牙5.4
*   **端口**：2x 雷电4，USB-A 3.2 Gen 1，USB-A 3.2 Gen 2，HDMI 2.1，3.5毫米音频插孔，microSD读卡器
*   **摄像头**：1080p红外摄像头
*   **电池**：70 Wh
*   **电源适配器**：100 W（Type-C）
*   **操作系统**：Windows 11 家庭版
*   **尺寸（宽x深x高）**：13.99 x 9.66 x 0.59 英寸
*   **重量**：3.42 磅
*   **测试配置价格**：1899.99美元

**宏碁Swift 16 AI的生产力性能**
评测样机搭载酷睿Ultra X7 358H CPU、32GB内存和1TB SSD。“Panther Lake” CPU拥有16个核心（4个性能核、8个能效核、4个低功耗能效核）和锐炫B390集成显卡，后者是X7/X9芯片相较于依赖英特尔核芯显卡的标准H系列芯片的一个关键卖点。

对比机型包括苹果MacBook Air (M4， 2025)、华硕Zenbook Duo (2026， 酷睿Ultra X9 388H)和戴尔16 Premium（酷睿Ultra 7 255H）。这一组合代表了中高端的生产力笔记本。

在Geekbench CPU测试中，宏碁的成绩中等。单核部分得分为2，789，与戴尔基本持平，但落后于华硕Zenbook（3，031），与MacBook（3，780）差距较大。其15，926分的多核成绩处于组内较低位置，虽然意外地领先于MacBook（14，924），但显著落后于华硕和戴尔。

在25GB文件传输测试中，宏碁以1，745 MB/s的速度领先，优于戴尔（1，531 MB/s），并将苹果和排名最后的华硕远远甩开。

最后，在4K转1080p的Handbrake视频转码测试中，宏碁用时4分25秒完成渲染，表现中等。华硕Zenbook（4:22）与之接近，戴尔最快（3:50），而MacBook最慢（4:52）。

通过连续运行10次Cinebench 2024模拟持续CPU负载的压力测试中，Swift 16初始得分为948，第二次运行时降至762分，但在随后的运行中稳定在900分左右，表明一旦风扇启动，没有明显的性能 throttling 现象。

**宏碁Swift 16 AI的显示屏**
OLED屏幕很少让人失望，这款也不例外，充分展现了该技术所著称的丰富色彩和深邃对比度。镜面屏的反光比预期更明显，但120Hz刷新率确保了滚动的顺滑。为节省电量，在电池供电时刷新率会自动切换至60Hz。需要注意的是，屏幕表面支持触控，但无法使用随附的手写笔，该手写笔仅在触控板上有效。

在此笔记本上观看Apple TV的《Silo》是一种享受。尽管该剧整体色调灰暗，很少给OLED屏展示色彩的机会，但在占据主导的阴影和昏暗场景中，它呈现了完美的细节，为这些场景增添了额外的深度和沉浸感，这是没有顶级显示屏无法体验到的。

宏碁的屏幕显示出卓越的色彩覆盖范围，完全覆盖DCI-P3色域，而华硕Zenbook为87.2%，MacBook为83.5%。对于OLED面板而言，其亮度也表现出色但不夸张，平均为364尼特，几乎与戴尔的367尼特持平，但落后于华硕的476尼特。

**宏碁Swift 16 AI的键盘和触控板**
这款宏碁的全尺寸键盘打字体验愉悦，反馈柔和而精准，键程充足。清晰的白色背光在各种光照条件下都易于辨识。数字小键盘较小，方向键尺寸也不统一，但其余布局没有意外。在MonkeyType打字测试中，首次尝试就以完美准确度达到了接近个人最佳的速度，约114词/分钟。

宏碁称其为全球最大的触感触控板，尺寸为6.9 x 4.3英寸。直观来说，它仅比苹果iPad mini略小一点。在日常使用中，我发现这个尺寸并非必需，因为手指更倾向于停留在触控板中央，除非使用多指滑动手势——得益于充足的空间，这种感觉非常奢华。触感反馈精准模拟了物理点击机制，并且可以在Windows触控板设置应用中调整强度。

这款触控板如此之大的真正原因是它支持MPP协议手写笔，宏碁在包装盒中附带了一支。其理念是可将触控板用作绘图板。触控板采用了与屏幕相同的16:10长宽比，以尽可能使操作直观。

在实际使用中，我并未发现手写笔特别有用。它的设计足够好——大小接近真实钢笔，支持倾斜感应，配备两个按钮，使用AAAA电池供电——但在触控板上书写和涂鸦感觉很别扭。手掌误触抑制并不总是有效，因此书写时我不得不避免将手掌放在表面，这让人感觉不自然。无论如何，我都很难书写清晰的字迹或在Photoshop中获得想要的笔触。总体而言，体验感觉不够精确且受限。对于偶尔签署电子文档可能有用，但对于创作者而言，使用USB绘图板或者直接购买平板电脑或二合一笔记本会是更好的选择。最好将这支手写笔视为一种附加福利，而非您会依赖其进行实际工作的功能。

**宏碁Swift 16 AI的音频**
宏碁通过掌托下方两个向下发射的扬声器提供了坚实的音频表现。在聆听Deep Chills的《Too Late for Love》原声音乐时，能听到清晰的乐器分离度和鼓点中明显的低音。人声在高音量时听起来有些紧绷，但DTS： X Ultra应用程序的均衡器在大多数情况下缓解了这一问题。均衡器对于低音至关重要；禁用后声音几乎平淡无奇。在我的听感中，“音乐”预设效果最佳，“电影”预设则听起来有点遥远。我尝试过自定义预设，但最终总是回归“音乐”预设——它的平衡恰到好处。

在播放《Silo》时，声音同样表现出色，人声细节丰富，虚拟环绕声效果逼真。不过，音量似乎在75%到80%之间就达到上限，超过这个范围，我的耳朵已无法分辨声音是否真的更大了。

**宏碁Swift 16 AI的可升级性**
八颗Torx螺丝固定着Swift 16 AI的底盖。卸下后，唯一的着力点位于显示屏转轴处，可以使用塑料撬片撬开固定卡扣。这个过程比预想的要费力得多。随后，我沿着边缘逐一撬开所有卡扣，小心翼翼地避免损坏它们。

可维护的组件仅限于M.2 SSD和电池，所有其他组件均为焊接或集成。在这类笔记本中，如此有限的可升级性并不令人意外。

**宏碁Swift 16 AI的电池续航**
电池测试包括网页浏览、运行OpenGL测试和视频流媒体播放，屏幕亮度设定为150尼特并连接Wi-Fi。

在12小时12分钟的续航测试中，宏碁的表现令人失望，仅超过了耗电量大的戴尔机型（7:15）。它比双屏华硕笔记本短了超过两小时，比MacBook短了三小时。虽然宏碁的续航时间并非不可用，但当前优秀产品的标准已经超过了它在这里展现的水平。

**宏碁Swift 16 AI的散热**
Swift 16 AI的冷却系统采用两个风扇，向显示屏转轴方向排气。在日常任务中，它们运行安静。即使在负载下噪音明显，但声级并不扰人，不会干扰他人。

在Cinebench压力测试期间，我们测量到键盘G和H键之间表面最高温度为92华氏度（约33.3摄氏度），触控板处为78华氏度（约25.6摄氏度），底部靠近转轴处为95华氏度（约35摄氏度）。同时，酷睿Ultra X7 358H CPU的平均运行温度为80摄氏度。总体而言，这是完全可以接受的热性能表现。

**宏碁Swift 16 AI的网络摄像头**
宏碁的1080p网络摄像头提供平均水平的画质。1080p分辨率看起来比几年前常见的720p更清晰，但仍显示柔和的细节。例如，我知道自己脸上有些柔和的皱纹，但它们并未被捕捉到（也许我该感到庆幸？）。在光线较暗的情况下，色彩和清晰度会下降，但您仍然能被识别。摄像头配备了用于面部识别的红外传感器和一个滑动式隐私快门。

**宏碁Swift 16 AI的软件和保修**
Acer Sense是最重要的应用程序，可通过顶部功能行的快捷键启动。该应用提供诊断、软件更新、优化的电池充电模式、蓝光过滤和自适应亮度设置。您还可以设置性能模式——静音、正常（默认）或性能。

进一步的个性化设置可在Acer User Sensing应用中找到，包括利用距离传感器实现离开时自动锁定或接近时唤醒，以及可选的在您移开视线时调暗屏幕、休息提醒和屏幕距离提醒。

宏碁预装了相当数量的试用应用，例如DropBox，我立即将其卸载。高端笔记本电脑上不应有任何臃肿软件。

宏碁为Swift 16 AI提供标准的一年保修。

**宏碁Swift 16 AI配置**
我们评测的Swift 16 AI型号为SF16-71T-75XY，将于2026年3月中旬在宏碁官方商店以1899.99美元的价格上市。它包括酷睿Ultra X7 358H处理器、英特尔锐炫B390集成显卡、32GB内存和1TB SSD。它还配备了所有型号标配的2880 x 1800 OLED触控屏。

宏碁还将通过百思买提供价格位于我们评测机型两侧的型号。售价1599美元的型号SF16-71T-70PN与我们的评测机型规格相同，但内存减半（16GB）；而售价1999美元的型号SF16-71T-97GV则将CPU升级至酷睿Ultra X9 388H。

所有型号均包含一个便携包（我们的评测样机未提供）和一个用于触控板的手写笔。

**总结**
宏碁Swift 16 AI是一款吸引人的高端超极本，适合愿意为基础款之外投入的购买者。其金属机身、生动的OLED显示屏、出色的键盘和表现不俗的扬声器共同营造了高端感，而宏碁实用的端口选择意味着大多数用户无需额外适配器。

虽然电池续航属于“良好，但非卓越”的范畴——考虑到OLED显示屏，这并不意外——但它落后于同类领先产品。性能表现良好，“Panther Lake”芯片带来了显著的代际提升，宏碁的热管理设计也相当出色。

“全球最大”的触感触控板是Swift 16 AI最独特的设计，但在实际使用中，其庞大的尺寸更多是一种新奇感而非必需品。随附的手写笔可以工作，但总体体验对我而言不够精确且别扭，很难视为核心卖点。最好将其视为额外福利，而非购买理由。

那么，Swift 16 AI是否物有所值？对于想要一台时尚、高端的16英寸Windows旅行伴侣的用户来说，它当然可以满足需求。它唯一的重大失误是过大的触控板与手写笔的组合，但这似乎并未带来显著的溢价。无论如何，以相似的价格，对于可以接受macOS的用户，苹果的15英寸MacBook Air仍然是我们首推的大屏超极本选择。尽管本次评测中我们只有M4型号与宏碁对比，但新的M5版Air在性能和续航方面可能会更加令人印象深刻。但对于忠实的Windows用户而言，宏碁Swift 16 AI是一款精致、有能力且能让大多数购买者满意的机器。

history.title: Meta reveals four new MTIA chips built for AI inference — to be released on a six-month cadence
history.description: All four chips have been developed in partnership with Broadcom and are scheduled for deployment within the next two years.

You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Meta today announced four successive generations of its in-house Meta Training and Inference Accelerator (MTIA) chips, all developed in partnership with Broadcom and scheduled for deployment within the next two years. “We’ve developed a competitive strategy for MTIA by prioritizing rapid, iterative development, reads Meta’s press release, along with an inference-first focus and frictionless adoption by building natively on industry standards.Go deeper with TH Premium: Chipmaking (Image credit: tsmc)A deeper look at the chipmaking supply chainTSMC's $165 billion U.S. investments examinedChina reportedly reverse-engineers EUV toolChina bets on DUV, as EUV blockade reshapes chipmakingThe four new chips are MTIA 300, 400, 450, and 500. MTIA 300 is already in production for ranking and recommendations training, while 400 is currently in lab testing ahead of data center deployment. MTIA 450 and 500 are targeted at AI inference and are scheduled for mass deployment in early 2027 and later in 2027, respectively. According to Meta's technical blog, from MTIA 300 through to MTIA 500, HBM bandwidth increases 4.5 times, and compute FLOPs increase 25 times.Meta says MTIA 450 doubles the HBM bandwidth of MTIA 400, describing it as “much higher than that of existing leading commercial products,” or, in other words, Nvidia’s H100 and H200. MTIA 500 then adds another 50% HBM bandwidth on top of 450, along with up to 80% more HBM capacity. Indeed, it’s HBM bandwidth and not raw FLOPs that are the main bottleneck during the decode phase of transformer inference, and mainstream GPUs are architected to maximize FLOPs for large-scale pre-training. This means they carry a cost and power overhead that Meta says is unnecessary for inference workloads.Swipe to scroll horizontallyRow 0 - Cell 0 MTIA 300MTIA 400MTIA 450MTIA 500Workload FocusR&R TrainingGeneralAI InferenceAI InferenceModule TDP800 W1,200 W1,400 W1,700 WHBM Bandwidth6.1 TB/s9.2 TB/s18.4 TB/s27.6 TB/sHBM Capacity216 GB288 GB288 GB384-512 GBMX4 Performance-12 PFLOPS21 PFLOPS30 PLOPSFP8/MX8 Performance1.2 PFLOPS6 PFLOPS7 PFLOPS10 PFLOPSBF16 Performance0.6 PLOPS3 PFLOPS3.5 PFLOPS5 PFLOPSMeta's approach also includes hardware acceleration for FlashAttention and mixture-of-experts feed-forward network computation, plus custom low-precision data types co-designed for inference. MTIA 450 supports MX4, delivering six times the MX4 FLOPs of FP16/BF16, with mixed low-precision computation that avoids the software overhead of data type conversion. In terms of eventual deployment, MTIA 400, 450, and 500 will all use the same chassis, rack, and network infrastructure, meaning each new chip generation drops into the existing physical footprint for easy interchange. It’s this modularity, Meta says, that’s behind MTIA’s roughly six-month chip cadence, which itself is much faster than the industry’s typical one-to-two year cycle.The software stack runs natively on PyTorch, vLLM, and Triton, with support for torch.compile and torch.export so that production models can be deployed simultaneously on both GPUs and MTIA without MTIA-specific rewrites. Meta said it has already deployed hundreds of thousands of MTIA chips across its apps for inference on organic content and ads.All this comes just two weeks after Meta disclosed a long-term, $100 billion AI infrastructure agreement with AMD, suggesting that there’s a broader effort at play to reduce dependence on Nvidia across different parts of Meta’s AI stack while keeping MTIA at the core of inference workloads.Article continues below You may like AMD and Meta strike $100 billion AI deal that includes 10% stock deal Microsoft introduces newest in-house AI chip — Maia 200 is faster than other bespoke Nvidia competitors, built on TSMC 3nm with 216GB of HBM3e Elon Musk reveals roadmap with nine-month cadence for new AI processor releases, beating Nvidia and AMD's yearly cadence Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: Meta宣布将发布四款专为AI推理打造的MTIA芯片——每半年推出一代
history.descriptionZh:
Meta今日宣布了其内部自研的Meta训练与推理加速器（MTIA）芯片的四代产品路线图。这四代芯片（MTIA 300, 400, 450, 500）均与博通合作开发，计划在未来两年内陆续部署。此举标志着Meta在构建自主AI基础设施、减少对英伟达等外部供应商依赖的战略上迈出了关键一步，尤其在其刚刚与AMD达成一项长期、价值可能高达1000亿美元的AI基础设施协议的背景下，其多元化布局意图更加明显。

Meta的核心策略是“推理优先”和基于行业标准的快速迭代开发。MTIA并非旨在替代GPU进行大规模预训练，而是专门针对AI推理工作负载（如内容推荐、广告排名）进行优化，追求更高的能效和更低的总体拥有成本。

**核心技术原理与创新点：**

1.  **架构针对性优化**：传统GPU（如英伟达H100/H200）为最大化大规模预训练的算力（FLOPs）而设计，这在推理阶段，特别是Transformer模型的解码阶段，会造成不必要的成本和功耗开销。MTIA反其道而行之，将设计重点放在解决推理的真实瓶颈——**内存带宽**上，通过大幅提升高带宽内存的带宽和容量来提升整体推理效率。
2.  **显著的HBM带宽跃升**：从MTIA 300到MTIA 500，HBM带宽提升了4.5倍，计算FLOPs提升了25倍。其中，**MTIA 450的HBM带宽被描述为“远高于现有领先商用产品”（即英伟达H100/H200）**，而MTIA 500在450的基础上再增加50%带宽，并最高提供80%的HBM容量提升。
3.  **定制低精度与硬件加速**：MTIA支持自定义低精度数据类型（如MX4），在硬件层面进行优化。MX4可提供相当于FP16/BF16六倍的算力，并通过混合低精度计算避免了数据类型转换的软件开销。此外，芯片还集成了对**FlashAttention和混合专家网络前馈计算**的硬件加速，直接提升关键AI算法的执行速度。
4.  **模块化设计与快速迭代**：MTIA 400、450和500将使用相同的机箱、机架和网络基础设施。这种模块化设计使得新旧芯片代际可以无缝替换，是Meta能够实现大约**6个月的芯片迭代周期**（远快于业界典型的1-2年）的关键。
5.  **软件生态无缝集成**：软件栈原生支持PyTorch、vLLM和Triton，并支持`torch.compile`和`torch.export`。这意味着现有的生产模型无需为MTIA进行重写，即可同时在GPU和MTIA上部署，极大降低了开发者的使用门槛和迁移成本。

**性能参数与对比分析：**

根据Meta公布的数据，四代芯片的性能演进如下：
*   **MTIA 300**：专注于排名与推荐训练，已投入生产。TDP 800W，HBM带宽6.1 TB/s。
*   **MTIA 400**：面向通用AI，正处于实验室测试阶段。TDP 1200W，HBM带宽9.2 TB/s，MX4算力12 PFLOPS。
*   **MTIA 450**：专为AI推理设计，计划2027年初大规模部署。TDP 1400W，**HBM带宽跃升至18.4 TB/s**，MX4算力21 PFLOPS。其带宽指标明确对标并超越英伟达旗舰产品。
*   **MTIA 500**：同为AI推理芯片，计划2027年后期部署。TDP 1700W，**HBM带宽再次提升至27.6 TB/s**，容量达384-512GB，MX4算力30 PFLOPS，是系列中性能最强的版本。

**技术影响与应用场景：**

MTIA芯片将深度集成到Meta庞大的应用生态中，包括Facebook、Instagram等，用于处理**有机内容推荐和广告的推理任务**。Meta已在其数据中心部署了“数十万颗”MTIA芯片。这一自研体系与外部GPU（来自英伟达、AMD等）形成互补：GPU继续主导计算密集的模型训练，而MTIA则在其拥有海量请求的推理场景中承担主力，以优化性能和成本。

**总结而言**，Meta通过MTIA项目展示了一条清晰的AI硬件发展路径：通过聚焦推理、极致优化内存带宽、打造软硬件协同的封闭生态，并利用模块化设计实现快速迭代，旨在为其核心社交与广告业务构建一个高效、可控且成本优化的专用AI算力底座。这不仅是Meta降低对单一供应商依赖的关键举措，也反映了全球科技巨头在AI竞赛中向底层硬件纵深发展的共同趋势。

history.title: Microsoft confirms next-gen Xbox, codenamed Project Helix, will be powered by custom AMD SoC and  feature 'FSR Diamond' — 'Xbox Mode' is also coming to Windows 11
history.description: The first official hardware details for Project Helix have been confirmed and Microsoft seems to be heavily leaning toward neural rendering, going as far as to unveil the next-gen FSR "Diamond" stack. Along with the AMD co-developed custom SoC, FSR will enable a significant bump in raytracing performance in the upcoming Xbox.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 19 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter At its GTC 2026 keynote, Microsoft took the wraps off Project Helix, announcing the first hardware details for the next-gen Xbox. The company reiterated how Helix will run both console and PC games, while confirming a partnership with AMD for its custom silicon, as expected. The biggest new info from the presentation was a surprise reveal for AMD's next-gen FSR "Diamond" stack that will be deeply integrated within Project Helix. The new "Xbox Mode" that debuted on Asus' Xbox Rog Ally devices will also be heading to Windows 11 this April.First, let's go over the list of now-confirmed building blocks for the upcoming Xbox console-PC hybrid. It will feature a custom SoC developed by the Red Team — likely featuring an RDNA 5 GPU — with a focus on next-gen raytracing performance. Microsoft namedrops next-gen DirectX, too, but it could be referring to a newer iteration of DirectX 12 that only ships on PC for now. (Image credit: Microsoft via IGN)Current-gen Xbox Series consoles already run a version of DirectX 12, but they don't support a lot of the latest features, namely "work graphs" that let the GPU itself drive code execution instead of relying on the CPU. The custom AMD silicon in Project Helix would support this, alongside neural texture compression. Microsoft is also combining DirectStorage and Zstd to accelerate SSD-to-GPU communication in the console.Article continues below You may like Microsoft confirms next-gen Xbox will play PC games AMD reveals next-gen Xbox could launch in 2027 Microsoft confirms Windows 11 26H1 will be for Arm devices only at launch Now we come to the juiciest bit, "FSR Next." It seems like Project Helix is being built around neural rendering, and the next-gen FSR stack will play a big part in achieving this. In the slide, Microsoft mentions next-gen ML-based upscaling and ray regeneration for both ray tracing and path tracing, which will be a first for consoles. The company even says Helix will support multi-frame-gen thanks to FSR Next.After the show, AMD graphics lead Jack Huynh revealed that FSR Next will be called FSR "Diamond" in a separate tweet. He also described Project Helix as a multi-year engineering collaboration between AMD and Microsoft. That certainly makes it sound like an evolving process, something that's more indefinite and supposed to get better over time.🚀 Big moment for the future of gaming.Thrilled to partner with @Xbox and @asha_shar on Project Helix, a multi-year deep co-engineering partnership driving next-gen performance, breakthrough graphics, and compatibility with your existing Xbox game library.Powering the… pic.twitter.com/twGyonqgQSMarch 11, 2026On the other hand, we could infer the above multi-year comment as simply a reference to the development time for the hardware. Huynh also mentions that FSR Diamond will be "natively optimized" for Helix and be a core tenet of the Xbox SDK. Upscaling has always been a key part of how some machines operate and how they're able to hit advertised performance numbers, but never quite so publicly before.Since the debut of current-gen Xbox and PlayStation devices, AI-based upscaling has taken over the industry with every new game shipping with some version of the tech. It's become a default part of the system requirements, so it makes sense that, years later, consoles — which are basically locked-down x86 computers, too — will also hop on the trend. After all, Project Helix is a PC-console hybrid.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsSpeaking of consoles that are also just PCs, the new "Xbox Mode" that debuted on Asus' Xbox Rog Ally devices will be heading to Windows 11 this April. To jog your memory: in this console-like mode, Windows limits background processes and telemetry to reduce overhead and squeeze as much out of the hardware as possible. Xbox Mode has already been available as part of the Insider program for months. (Image credit: Microsoft)It was previously known as Xbox Full Screen Experience (FSE), but the name has been updated since. Xbox Mode will roll out to "select markets" first and likely represents the first step in the PC-console unification Microsoft hopes to achieve by the time Project Helix's launches. The Xbox Play Anywhere program, which allows for cross-progression between platforms, was mentioned at the show as another sign of this future.Speaking of which, "alpha" kits for Project Helix will begin heading out to relevant parties in 2027, which implies a late 2027 launch at best, but component shortages could push that back. A 2027 release would be in line with the typical seven-year cycle that consoles follow, as the Xbox Series S|X debuted in Q4 2020. The only thing left now is to wait and see what kind of response Sony is preparing with its PS6.Even though the base hardware and neural rendering-first approach would likely be similar between Sony and Microsoft, the former has no interest in appealing to PC anymore. In contrast, Microsoft is almost forcing PC and console together to eliminate the need for even picking a platform. It even told developers at GDC to "build for PC" going forward, further implying that Project Helix is just moonlighting as a console. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: 微软确认新一代Xbox（代号Project Helix）将搭载定制AMD芯片，并具备'FSR Diamond'功能——Windows 11也将迎来'Xbox模式'
history.descriptionZh:
在GTC 2026大会上，微软揭晓了其下一代游戏平台“Project Helix”的首批硬件细节，明确其将作为兼具主机与PC游戏能力的混合设备登场。正如业界预期，该项目确认将与AMD合作开发定制芯片。然而，发布会最引人注目的亮点是AMD下一代超分辨率技术FSR “Diamond”的意外亮相，该技术栈将深度集成于Helix项目中。此外，最初在华硕Xbox Rog Ally设备上亮相的“Xbox模式”也确认将于今年四月登陆Windows 11。

**核心硬件架构与技术基础**
Project Helix已确认的核心构建模块包括一款由AMD设计的定制化片上系统（SoC），其GPU部分很可能基于下一代RDNA 5架构，并着重提升下一代光线追踪性能。微软也提及了“下一代DirectX”，这可能是指目前仅限PC的、更新迭代的DirectX 12版本。

当前世代的Xbox Series主机虽已运行DirectX 12，但缺乏许多最新特性，例如允许GPU自主驱动代码执行而非依赖CPU的“工作图”技术。Project Helix的定制AMD芯片将支持此项技术，并引入**神经纹理压缩**。此外，微软还将**DirectStorage API与Zstd压缩算法**相结合，旨在加速主机内SSD与GPU之间的数据通信，减少加载时间与卡顿。

**革命性的渲染创新：FSR “Diamond”与神经渲染**
本次披露最具颠覆性的信息围绕“FSR Next”展开。Project Helix似乎正围绕**神经渲染**理念构建，而下一代FSR技术栈将是实现这一目标的关键。根据演示幻灯片，微软提到了基于机器学习的新一代超分辨率技术，以及专为光线追踪与路径追踪设计的**光线再生**功能——这将是游戏主机上的首创。公司甚至表示，借助FSR Next，Helix将支持**多帧生成**技术。

会后，AMD图形部门负责人Jack Huynh在社交媒体上透露，FSR Next的正式名称将为FSR **“Diamond”** 。他将Project Helix描述为AMD与微软之间一项“多年的深度协同工程合作”，暗示这是一个持续演进、随时间不断优化的过程。Huynh同时指出，FSR Diamond将为Helix进行“原生优化”，并成为Xbox软件开发工具包的核心原则。尽管超分辨率技术早已成为许多设备达到标称性能的关键，但如此公开地将其置于硬件设计的核心地位尚属首次。

**行业趋势与平台融合战略**
自本世代Xbox和PlayStation主机发布以来，基于AI的超分辨率技术已席卷游戏行业，成为新游戏的标配功能。因此，作为本质上锁定的x86计算机，游戏主机拥抱这一趋势合情合理，更何况Helix本身定位就是PC-主机混合体。

与此战略相呼应，最初为华硕掌机设计的“Xbox模式”将登陆Windows 11。该模式通过限制后台进程与遥测数据收集来降低系统开销，从而在PC硬件上榨取尽可能多的游戏性能。此模式此前已在Insider测试项目中提供数月，它被认为是微软在Project Helix发布前，推动PC与主机体验统一的第一步。发布会上提及的**Xbox Play Anywhere**（支持跨平台进度共享）项目也是这一未来愿景的体现。

**发布时间线与行业影响**
据悉，Project Helix的“alpha”开发套件将于2027年开始向相关方分发，这暗示其最早可能在2027年底发布，但潜在的零部件短缺可能推迟这一时间表。若按此计划，将与游戏主机约七年的换代周期相符（Xbox Series S|X于2020年第四季度发布）。

尽管索尼的PS6在基础硬件和以神经渲染为先导的设计思路上可能与微软相似，但双方战略显着不同：索尼对吸引PC市场并无兴趣，而微软则正强力推动PC与主机的融合，旨在消除玩家选择平台的必要。微软甚至在游戏开发者大会上鼓励开发者“为PC而构建”，这进一步暗示Project Helix在本质上是一台扮演着主机角色的PC。

**总结**
Project Helix代表了微软重塑游戏生态的宏大愿景，其核心是通过与AMD的深度合作，将定制高性能SoC、革命性的FSR Diamond神经渲染技术、以及系统级的存储与数据流优化融为一体。这不仅旨在实现主机性能的世代飞跃，更致力于彻底模糊PC与主机之间的界限，通过软件体验（如Xbox模式、Play Anywhere）与硬件设计的双重融合，为玩家和开发者构建一个统一的平台。它的到来，预示着游戏行业向以神经渲染为中心、计算平台趋同的未来迈出了关键一步。

history.title: How to use Precision Boost Overdrive and Curve Optimizer to improve Ryzen CPU performance
history.description: Using Precision Boost Overdrive and Curve Optimizer, you can fine tune your processor’s voltage and power limits to achieve better efficiency and sustained boost speeds.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter AMD’s Precision Boost Overdrive (PBO) and Curve Optimizer (CO) are two powerful tools that can help squeeze additional performance out of Ryzen desktop processors. Primarily a feature that removes power limits but is classified as overclocking, PBO can increase power, current, and voltage limits to boost clock speeds for improved performance. Since it works in real-time, it allows the CPU to run faster during heavy workloads without requiring manual overclocking. If you want more detail on manual overclocking or on stress testing approaches, head to our How to Overclock your CPU article, but for the basics of using AMD's Ryzen Master for PBO and CO, you are in the right place.CO is a feature available within PBO that can be used to adjust the voltage-frequency curve of all or individual cores. As modern desktop processors ship with slightly higher stock voltages to ensure stability, reducing the voltage leads to less power consumption and lower heat generation while sustaining their rated clock speeds.Given the right conditions, this improved efficiency allows the CPU to maintain higher boost clock speeds for longer periods since it is less likely to hit the thermal or power limits. The result is quieter operation, lower temperatures, and in some cases, better real-world performance.Article continues below You may like Our testing shows the Ryzen 7 9800X3D can match the pricier Ryzen 7 9850X3D with simple PBO settings MSI adds auto BCLK overclocking to X870 MAX motherboards Binning results from 13 Ryzen 7 9850X3Ds prove that the CPU is purely an overclocked 9800X3D from the factory Having said that, undervolting comes with potential drawbacks. Excessive voltage reduction can lead to system instability, random freezes, crashes, or blue screens (BSOD), especially under heavy workloads. Finding the right settings for stable operations can also be time-consuming, often requiring hours of stress testing to identify the right balance between voltage and performance.In certain cases, lowering the voltage can reduce performance, as the CPU may struggle to maintain its intended clock speeds, resulting in slower processing. Results may also vary due to the silicon lottery, meaning some chips can handle more aggressive voltage reductions and remain stable compared to others.While undervolting can be achieved through your motherboard’s BIOS, AMD’s Ryzen Master software offers all the necessary tools to fine-tune your CPU, including Precision Boost Overdrive and Curve Optimizer. Here’s a step-by-step guide on how to use them:SPONSOREDGigabyte X870E AORUS MASTER X3D ICE: was $389.99 now $369.99 at AmazonEnjoy one-click acceleration and boost your PCs performance up to 25% with X3D Turbo Mode 2.0 and a built-in AI model that optimizes 3D processor parameters in real-time while Infinite Memory Performance X3D boosts DDR5 performance. Faster, fiercer, more precise gaming at your fingertips.View DealDownload and install AMD Ryzen MasterHead over to the official AMD website and visit the Ryzen Master download page. Click on the download button for Ryzen Master and grab the latest version that is compatible with your operating system and CPU generation. Once the installation file is downloaded, locate the file and double-click on it to start the installation process. Follow the setup wizard to install Ryzen Master on your system.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsAfter the setup finishes, the installer may ask you to restart your system. Once you boot into Windows, launch the Ryzen Master software. Heed the warning that appears each time you open the software; overclocking voids your warranty, and all forms of PBO are strictly classified as overclocking.The software will open in Basic View with limited controls and a dashboard showing key system information, including your processor model, clock speeds, temperature, voltage, and various overclocking profiles. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)To access performance tuning features such as Precision Boost Overdrive and Curve Optimizer, switch to Advanced View by clicking the button at the bottom of the interface. What to read next CPU scaling with DLSS AMD Ryzen 7 9850X3D review: The world's fastest gaming processor, again AMD Ryzen 7 9850X3D vs Ryzen 7 9800X3D faceoff (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Enable Precision Boost Overdrive (PBO)Enabling Advanced View gives access to a wide range of controls along with system monitoring tools. On the top left are multiple performance profiles, including Creator Mode, Game Mode, and two additional options, all of which can be tweaked.You can also adjust additional parameters, including PPT (Package Power Tracking), EDC (Electrical Design Current), and TDC (Thermal Design Current) with the different PBO modes.Under Control Mode, you can select Precision Boost Overdrive, which has basic automated EDC, TDC, and PPT values. These values are automatically set to motherboard limits, as the motherboard firmware automatically determines safe values based on its power delivery capabilities.For more granular control, you can choose Auto Overclocking mode, which allows you to define a Boost Override CPU value that increases the clock speed of the processor beyond the rated boost clock, but this maxes out at +200 MHz. This also unlocks the PBO Scalar option, which increases the amount of time the processor runs at a higher boost clock.Once you select your desired options, hit Apply and Test at the bottom. The Ryzen Master software will now apply your settings and run a quick test to check for system stability. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Once PBO is active, the CPU should begin using any additional headroom to boost more aggressively during heavy workloads.Using Curve Optimizer (CO)With Precision Boost Overdrive enabled, you can now fine-tune the processor using Curve Optimizer. As mentioned earlier, this feature allows you to adjust the voltage-frequency curve of the CPU cores, or in simpler terms, reduce the voltage required for a given clock speed. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Select the Curve Optimizer section from the menu on the left. You can choose between applying a curve adjustment to all cores at the same time or tuning each core individually. We recommend applying the same voltage offset to all cores as it is less tedious and make sure that Eco Mode is disabled.Since our goal is to apply a negative curve offset, begin by selecting All Cores to reveal a new section called CO All Core Value. Next, enter a negative value. A good starting point would be –10 before gradually working your way to a lower value, as we don’t want to push the processor beyond its stability limits.After entering the value, click on Start Optimizing. The Ryzen Master software will run a short validation test to check for stability issues. If the system remains stable, you can save the configuration as a profile.Testing system stabilityWhile Ryzen Master runs a quick validation test, it is best to perform a long stress test to confirm that the undervolt is truly stable, as real-world workloads can behave differently from short synthetic tests. You can test for system stability by running a CPU-demanding game or a benchmark like Cinebench R23 or Prime 95.Validate your results by running the benchmark before applying a negative voltage offset and comparing the results after applying the offset. Ideally, the benchmark score after undervolting the CPU should improve by a small margin. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Make sure that you also monitor the CPU temperatures, thermal throttling, clock speeds, and system stability during testing. In the event of a system crash, freezing, or hardware error, the curve offset might be too aggressive. Reduce the negative offset slightly and repeat the testing process until you find a stable configuration. With the right settings dialed in, Precision Boost Overdrive and Curve Optimizer can help unlock additional performance and reduce temperatures on your Ryzen processor. By carefully offsetting the voltage, you are allowing the CPU to sustain higher boost clocks for longer during demanding workloads.Remember, patience is key. Every processor behaves differently, and the ideal settings for one system may not work for another. Take your time to make gradual adjustments and test for stability before settling on a final configuration.

history.titleZh: 如何运用精准增压超频与曲线优化器提升锐龙处理器性能
history.descriptionZh:
AMD的Precision Boost Overdrive（PBO，精确增压超频）和Curve Optimizer（CO，曲线优化器）是两项用于提升Ryzen桌面处理器性能的重要工具。本文详细介绍了它们的工作原理、设置方法以及潜在风险，为希望通过软件调优获得更好能效表现的用户提供了实用指南。

**技术原理与创新点**
PBO本质上是一种解除预设功耗限制的自动超频功能。它通过实时动态提升处理器的功耗、电流和电压上限，使CPU在重负载下能够运行在更高的时钟频率，从而获得性能提升。与传统手动超频不同，PBO无需用户锁定频率，而是由系统根据工作负载和散热条件自动决策。

Curve Optimizer是集成在PBO框架内的一项更精细的调优功能。它的核心创新在于允许用户调整CPU核心的电压-频率曲线（v/f曲线）。由于现代处理器出厂时为保证稳定性通常会预设偏高的电压，通过CO施加负向偏移（降压），可以在维持标称频率的同时降低电压。这直接减少了功耗和发热，在理想情况下，更低的温度和功耗使得CPU更不容易触及热限制或功耗墙，从而能够更长时间地维持高加速频率。最终效果是实现更安静的运行、更低的温度，并在许多情况下提升实际应用性能。

**性能影响与潜在风险**
合理使用PBO和CO可以显著提升能效。例如，测试表明，通过简单的PBO设置，Ryzen 7 9800X3D的性能可以媲美更昂贵的Ryzen 7 9850X3D。然而，降压超频并非没有代价。过度降低电压会导致系统不稳定，出现随机冻结、崩溃或蓝屏，尤其在重负载下。寻找稳定的设置通常需要耗费大量时间进行压力测试。此外，在某些情况下，电压过低反而会导致性能下降，因为CPU可能无法维持目标频率。最终效果也受“芯片体质”（硅晶圆 lottery）影响，不同处理器的降压潜力差异很大。

**配置方法与操作步骤**
用户可以通过主板BIOS或AMD官方的Ryzen Master软件进行设置。Ryzen Master提供了集成的图形化界面，以下是使用该软件的基本步骤：
1.  **安装与启动**：从AMD官网下载并安装最新版Ryzen Master，启动后需注意超频可能导致保修失效的警告。
2.  **切换至高级视图**：软件默认打开基础视图，需点击切换至“高级视图”以访问PBO和CO等高级控制选项。
3.  **启用Precision Boost Overdrive**：
    *   在控制模式中选择“Precision Boost Overdrive”，系统将自动采用主板设定的安全功耗（PPT）、电流（EDC/TDC）限制。
    *   对于更精细的控制，可选择“自动超频”模式，此模式允许用户设置最大+200 MHz的频率提升偏移量（Boost Override），并解锁PBO Scalar选项以延长高频率的维持时间。
    *   设置后点击“应用并测试”，软件会进行快速稳定性验证。
4.  **配置Curve Optimizer**：
    *   在PBO启用后，从左侧菜单选择“Curve Optimizer”。
    *   建议初学者选择“All Cores”（所有核心），并确保节能模式（Eco Mode）已关闭。
    *   在“CO All Core Value”中输入一个负值（如-10），即表示施加负电压偏移。应从较小值开始逐步尝试。
    *   点击“开始优化”进行简短验证。
5.  **系统稳定性测试**：Ryzen Master的快速验证不足以确保长期稳定。必须进行长时间的压力测试，例如使用Cinebench R23或Prime95，并对比降压前后的 benchmark 分数。理想情况下，降压后分数应有小幅提升。测试中需密切监控温度、频率和系统稳定性。若出现不稳定现象，应逐步减小负偏移值并重复测试，直至找到稳定配置。

**总结**
综上所述，AMD的PBO和CO为Ryzen处理器用户提供了强大且相对便捷的性能挖掘工具。通过智能地解除功耗限制并精细优化电压-频率关系，用户可以在降低系统温度和噪音的同时，潜在提升处理器在重负载下的持续性能。然而，这个过程需要耐心和细致的测试，因为每颗CPU的体质不同，不存在通用的最优设置。对于愿意投入时间调试的用户，这两项工具无疑是释放Ryzen芯片额外潜力的关键。
